name: Demand Letter
description: Formal demand letter for payment, performance, or cease and desist

sections.name: Letterhead
Introduction
sections.promptInstruction: Write a formal opening paragraph that: (1) Identifies the sender as counsel for the client, (2) States the purpose of the letter, (3) Sets a professional but firm tone. Keep it brief - 2-3 sentences.

sections.name: Factual Background
sections.promptInstruction: Describe the relevant facts that give rise to the demand. Be specific about dates, agreements, and actions taken. Present facts objectively but from the client's perspective. Use placeholders like [SPECIFIC DATE] or [SPECIFIC AMOUNT] for facts to be filled in.

sections.name: Legal Basis
sections.promptInstruction: Explain the legal basis for the demand. Cite applicable California statutes, case law, or contractual provisions. Be authoritative but not overly aggressive. This section establishes that the client has valid legal claims.

sections.name: Specific Demand
sections.promptInstruction: State the specific demand clearly. What exactly must the recipient do? By when? Include the amount if monetary, or specific actions if non-monetary. Make the demand clear and unambiguous. Reference the response deadline from the variables.

sections.name: Consequences of Non-Compliance
sections.promptInstruction: Explain the consequences if the recipient fails to comply. Reference potential legal action, damages, attorney fees, and costs. Be firm but professional - avoid threats that could be seen as improper. Mention that this letter may be used as evidence of bad faith if litigation ensues.

sections.name: Closing
